title: At Night
content:
Home, home from the horizon far and clear,
         Hither the soft wings sweep;
    Flocks of the memories of the day draw near
         The dovecote doors of sleep.

    O which are they that come through sweetest light
         Of all these homing birds?
    Which with the straightest and the swiftest flight?
         Your words to me, your words!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alice Christiana Gertrude Thompson Meynell, titled "At Night", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
